message:
feat: faker mode — synthetic replacement instead of [label] markers

Adds `markerPresets.faker()` — a built-in MarkerStrategy that emits
realistic-looking fake values (Alice Anderson, alice.anderson@example.com,
+1-555-0100, 4111-1111-1111-XXXX) instead of the default [private_email]
markers. Stateful per-instance: same input text always maps to the same
fake within the filter's lifetime, so "Alice" mentioned twice yields
"Alice Anderson" both times.

Use case: prod-shaped test fixtures, sales demos, customer support
transcript sharing with vendors — anywhere downstream code expects
PII-shaped data even after redaction. Tonic.ai charges money for this;
this is the OSS local-first version.

Per-category fakes use reserved/test ranges:
- private_phone: NANPA 555-01XX (reserved for fiction)
- private_url: example.com (RFC 2606)
- account_number: 4111-1111-1111-XXXX (test Visa BIN)
- private_address: Springfield, IL stub
- secret: NOT faked (footgun — leaking a credible-looking token is worse
  than just leaking [secret] tags)

API: zero new options on create(). markers already accepts a function;
faker is just a built-in factory that returns one. Re-export from both
browser and node entries.

Tests:
- 14 pure-logic unit tests in tests/unit/marker-presets.test.js
- end-to-end integration test in tests/native/integration/filter-faker.test.js
  verifies real model spans flow through the faker function correctly,
  including same-text consistency across multiple mentions

files.patch:
@@ -91,6 +91,18 @@ const filter = await PrivacyFilter.create({ presets: ["secrets"] });
 // npm tokens, PEM private-key headers. All severity "block".
 ```
 
+Faker mode — emit realistic fakes instead of `[private_email]` markers (so downstream validators / templates / pipelines still see PII-shaped data):
+
+```ts
+import { PrivacyFilter, markerPresets } from "textsift";
+
+const filter = await PrivacyFilter.create({ markers: markerPresets.faker() });
+await filter.redact("Hi Alice, email alice@example.com, phone +1-555-0123");
+// → "Hi Alice Anderson, email alice.anderson@example.com, phone +1-555-0100"
+//   Same input text → same fake within the filter's lifetime
+//   (so "Alice" appearing twice yields "Alice Anderson" both times)
+```
+
 Batch inputs, custom markers, per-category enabling — see the [API reference](https://teamchong.github.io/textsift/api/).
 
 ## Measured numbers
